Thought 3: The sickle is a crucial piece of evidence. Max, as an art dealer with a collection of historical farming artifacts, owns a sickle. This directly connects him to the type of weapon used in the murder. Anthony, on the other hand, is an artist whose conflict is ideological and artistic; there is no mention of him having access to or any connection with a sickle. Furthermore, Max was the last person confirmed to be with Dwayne in the very car where the body was found, placing him at the scene. His motive is also strong and immediate—Dwayne was about to publicly humiliate him by exposing a fake artifact, which would ruin his reputation. Anthony's motive, while present, seems less direct and more about long-standing disagreements. The gold paint on Anthony's clothes is a red herring; it's unusual but not linked to the crime. Max's unease when questioned and his admission of being the last with Dwayne make him the more likely suspect.
Action 3: Finish[1]
